description:
You are acting as a DevSecOps engineer who must enforce a “policy-as-code” rule set against an application log file.
The scenario is already partially set up for you:

INITIAL SETUP (already present when the session starts)
1. A directory /home/user/logs/
2. A file  /home/user/logs/app.log  that contains one log entry per line in the exact format  
   ISO-8601-timestamp␣SEVERITY␣Message‐text  
   (see the sample lines in the list below).

POLICY TO ENFORCE
• Any log entry whose SEVERITY is either ERROR or WARN **and** whose Message-text (case-insensitive) contains at least one of the following key phrases is considered a violation:  
  - “failed login”  
  - “unauthorized”  
  - “xss”  

DELIVERABLES YOU MUST CREATE
A. /home/user/output/violation_report.log  
   • The directory /home/user/output/ must be created if it does not yet exist.  
   • Each violating log line, after filtering, must be rewritten to the format:  
     timestamp|SEVERITY|Message-text  
     …where “|” is a literal vertical bar.  
   • Lines must appear in the same chronological order as in app.log.  
   • No blank or duplicate lines; the file must end with a single trailing newline.

B. /home/user/output/violation_summary.json  
   • A single-line, minified JSON object with the following exact keys and value types:  
     {
       "total_violations": <integer>,
       "by_severity": {
         "ERROR": <integer>,
         "WARN": <integer>
       }
     }  
   • The counts must match the number of entries you placed in violation_report.log.

REQUIREMENTS & HINTS
1. You are free to use any POSIX shell tools (grep, awk, tee, etc.).  
2. Show intermediate console output if helpful; only the two deliverable files will be graded.  
3. The task will be automatically verified, so adhere strictly to the required file paths, names, formats and order.

SAMPLE ENTRIES CURRENTLY INSIDE /home/user/logs/app.log (for reference)
2023-08-21T14:32:07Z ERROR Failed login for user 'admin' from 10.0.0.23  
2023-08-21T14:33:58Z INFO  User 'alice' authenticated successfully from 10.0.0.11  
2023-08-21T14:34:12Z WARN  Unauthorized access attempt to /secure/data by user 'bob'  
2023-08-21T14:35:47Z ERROR Reflected XSS attempt detected in parameter 'q'  
2023-08-21T14:36:02Z DEBUG Cache miss for key 'session_123'  
2023-08-21T14:36:15Z INFO  User 'alice' logged out  
2023-08-21T14:37:00Z WARN  Rate limit exceeded for IP 10.0.0.23  

Your job is to:
• Create the output directory if needed.  
• Build a one-liner or multi-step pipeline that (possibly in parallel using pipes) extracts the violating lines, transforms them into the required bar-separated format, writes them to violation_report.log, and simultaneously counts them by severity so you can output violation_summary.json.  

Once both output files exist with the exact expected content, the task is complete.
truth:
Existing files before the task starts
-------------------------------------
1. /home/user/logs/app.log  
   Content (with trailing newline after every line):

2023-08-21T14:32:07Z ERROR Failed login for user 'admin' from 10.0.0.23
2023-08-21T14:33:58Z INFO User 'alice' authenticated successfully from 10.0.0.11
2023-08-21T14:34:12Z WARN Unauthorized access attempt to /secure/data by user 'bob'
2023-08-21T14:35:47Z ERROR Reflected XSS attempt detected in parameter 'q'
2023-08-21T14:36:02Z DEBUG Cache miss for key 'session_123'
2023-08-21T14:36:15Z INFO User 'alice' logged out
2023-08-21T14:37:00Z WARN Rate limit exceeded for IP 10.0.0.23

(7 lines total)

Required files after successful completion
------------------------------------------
1. /home/user/output/violation_report.log  
   Exact content (including final trailing newline):

2023-08-21T14:32:07Z|ERROR|Failed login for user 'admin' from 10.0.0.23
2023-08-21T14:34:12Z|WARN|Unauthorized access attempt to /secure/data by user 'bob'
2023-08-21T14:35:47Z|ERROR|Reflected XSS attempt detected in parameter 'q'

2. /home/user/output/violation_summary.json  
   Exact content (one single line, newline-terminated):

{"total_violations":3,"by_severity":{"ERROR":2,"WARN":1}}

Permissions
-----------
• The parent directory /home/user/output/ should exist with permissions 755 (drwxr-xr-x user:user).  
• Both deliverable files should have mode 644 (-rw-r--r--).

Automated verification will fail if:
• The directory or either file is missing.  
• Lines, counts, ordering, separators or whitespace deviate from the specification.  
• The JSON is not minified or lacks the required keys.
